preserved\")\n311\t\t})\n312\t\n313\t\tt.Run(\"Header with many signed headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n314\t\t\t// Test with a large number of signed headers\n315\t\t\tmanyHeaders := TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing verifies that parsing and rebuilding\n334\t// the Authorization header produces the same result.\n335\tfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing(t *testing.T) {\n336\t\ttestHeaders := []string{\n337\t\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, testHeaders {\n342\t\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"Round-trip test %d\", i+1), func(t *testing.T) {\n343\t\t\t\t// Parse the header\n344\t\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(originalHeader)\n345\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n346\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header: %v\", err)\n347\t\t\t\t\treturn\n348\t\t\t\t}\n349\t\n350\t\t\t\t// Reconstruct the header from parsed components\n351\t\t\t\t// The {\n364\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse reconstructed header: %v\", err)\n365\t\t\t\t\treturn\n366\t\t\t\t}\n367\t\n368\t\t\t\t// Verify all components match\n369\t\t\t\tif parsedAgain.AccessKey != parsed.AccessKey {\n370\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AccessKey changed during round-trip: %s -> %s\", parsed.AccessKey, parsedAgain.AccessKey)\n371\t\t\t\t}\n372\t\n373\t\t\t\tif parsedAgain.Signature != handling pipeline.\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Create test credentials\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\thandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"Real AWS SigV4 signed request preserves header\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a properly signed request using real AWS SigV4\n\t\treq := createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n\n\t\t// Capture the Authorization header before sending\n\t\toriginalAuth := req.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Sending request with Authorization header: %s\", truncateForLog(originalAuth))\n\n\t\t// Create response recorder\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t\t// Serve the request\n\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// The request should be processed (may succeed or fail auth, but should not error on header parsing)\n\t\tif w.Code == http.StatusBadRequest || w.Code == http.StatusInternalServerError {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Request failed with status %d, indicating possible header parsing issue\", w.Code)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Response body: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Now parse the original header to verify it was well-formed\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(originalAuth)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse the Authorization header we just created: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify all components are present\n\t\tif parsed.Algorithm != \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Algorithm mismatch: expected AWS4-HMAC-SHA256, got %s\", parsed.Algorithm)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif parsed.AccessKey != \"TESTACCESSKEY\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AccessKey mismatch: expected TESTACCESSKEY, got %s\", parsed.AccessKey)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif parsed.Signature == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature is empty (header may have been truncated)\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(parsed.SignedHeaders) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders is empty (header may have been truncated)\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved intact through request pipeline\")\n\t\tt.Logf(\"  Algorithm: %s\", parsed.Algorithm)\n\t\tt.Logf(\"  AccessKey: %s\", parsed.AccessKey)\n\t\tt.Logf(\"  Signature: %s (length: %d)\", truncateForLog(parsed.Signature), len(parsed.Signature))\n\t\tt.Logf(\"  SignedHeaders: %v\", parsed.SignedHeaders)\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestAuthorizationHeaderEdgeCases tests edge cases for Authorization header handling\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderEdgeCases(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Header with maximum length signature\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a header with a maximum-length signature (64 hex chars = 256 bits)\n\t\tmaxSig := strings.Repeat(\"abcd\", 16) // 64 characters\n\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\", maxSig)\n\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with max-length signature: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif parsed.Signature != maxSig {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature truncated: expected length %d, got length %d\", len(maxSig), len(parsed.Signature))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Maximum-length signature preserved: %d characters\", len(parsed.Signature))\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Header with special characters in signature\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// AWS signatures are hex, so test with all valid hex characters\n\t\tspecialSig := \"abcdef0123456789ABCDEF0123456789abcdef0123456789ABCDEF0123456789\"\n\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\", specialSig)\n\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with mixed-case signature: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif parsed.Signature != specialSig {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Special character signature not preserved: expected %q, got %q\", specialSig, parsed.Signature)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Special character signature preserved\")\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Header with many signed headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test with a large number of signed headers\n\t\tmanyHeaders := []string{\"host\", \"x-amz-date\", \"x-amz-content-sha256\", \"content-type\", \"content-length\", \"x-amz-security-token\", \"x-amz-user-agent\"}\n\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(manyHeaders, \";\")\n\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=%s, Signature=abc123\", signedHeadersStr)\n\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with many signed headers: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(parsed.SignedHeaders) != len(manyHeaders) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders count mismatch: expected %d, got %d\", len(manyHeaders), len(parsed.SignedHeaders))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Many signed headers preserved: %d headers\", len(parsed.SignedHeaders))\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing verifies that parsing and rebuilding\n// the Authorization header produces the same result.\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestHeaders := []string{\n\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\",\n\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20260714/us-west-2/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=content-type;host;x-amz-date;x-amz-security-token, Signature=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, originalHeader := range testHeaders {\n\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"Round-trip test %d\", i+1), func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Parse the header\n\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(originalHeader)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Reconstruct the header from parsed components\n\t\t\t// The format is: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=..., SignedHeaders=..., Signature=...\n\t\t\treconstructed := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s/%s/%s/%s/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=%s, Signature=%s\",\n\t\t\t\tparsed.AccessKey,\n\t\t\t\tparsed.CredentialDate,\n\t\t\t\tparsed.Region,\n\t\t\t\tparsed.Service,\n\t\t\t\tstrings.Join(parsed.SignedHeaders, \";\"),\n\t\t\t\tparsed.Signature,\n\t\t\t)\n\n\t\t\t// Parse the reconstructed header\n\t\t\tparsedAgain, err := ParseAuthHeader(reconstructed)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse reconstructed header: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify all components match\n\t\t\tif parsedAgain.AccessKey != parsed.AccessKey {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AccessKey changed during round-trip: %s -> %s\", parsed.AccessKey, parsedAgain.AccessKey)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif parsedAgain.Signature != parsed.Signature {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature changed during round-trip: %s -> %s\", parsed.Signature, parsedAgain.Signature)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif len(parsedAgain.SignedHeaders) != len(parsed.SignedHeaders) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders count changed during round-trip: %d -> %d\", len(parsed.SignedHeaders), len(parsedAgain.SignedHeaders))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header unchanged through parse/reconstruct cycle\")\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// Helper function to truncate long strings for logging\nfunc truncateForLog(s string) string {\n\tif len(s) > 80 {\n\t\treturn s[:40] + \"...\" + s[len(s)-37:]\n\t}\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughInStreamingMode tests Authorization header\n// passthrough for streaming uploads (which use a slightly different auth format)\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughInStreamingMode(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Chunked streaming auth header format\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Streaming uploads use a slightly different format with the chunk signature\n\t\t// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=..., SignedHeaders=..., Signature=..., x-amz-content-sha256=STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD\n\t\t// The actual chunk signatures come in subsequent headers\n\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=abc123\"\n\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse streaming auth header: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Streaming auth header missing x-amz-content-sha256 in signed headers\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Streaming auth header format preserved correctly\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestAuthorizationHeaderExactPassthrough verifies that ARMOR receives the EXACT same\n// Authorization header byte-for-byte that was sent by the client.\n//\n// This test addresses the core requirement: \"Verifies ARMOR receives the exact same header value\"\n// by capturing the Authorization header inside the authentication layer and comparing it\n// to the original value.\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderExactPassthrough(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Create test credentials\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg format with most common signed headers\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"AWS SigV4 with content-type and additional headers\",\n\t\t\tauthHeader: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 format without spaces after commas\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tt.Log(\"Testing Exact Authorization Header Passthrough\")\n\tt.Log(\"This test verifies that ARMOR receives the EXACT same Authorization header\")\n\tt.Log(\"byte-for-byte that was sent by the client, with no modification or corruption.\")\n\tt.Log(\"\")\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n\n\t\t\t// Create request with the Authorization header\n\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", tc.authHeader)\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t\t// {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization header was modified during passthrough!\")\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original length: %d\", len(originalAuth))\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured length: %d\", len(capturedAuthHeader))\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:  %q\", originalAuth)\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured: %q\", capturedAuthHeader)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Find the first difference\n\t\t\t\tminLen := capturedAuthHeader[i] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"First difference at byte %d: original[%d]=%c (0x%02x), captured[%d]=%c (0x%02x)\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ti, i, originalAuth[i], originalAuth[i],\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ti, capturedAuthHeader[i], capturedAuthHeader[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header passed through intact (byte-for-byte match)\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Header length: %d bytes\", len(capturedAuthHeader))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Algorithm: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  No modification, truncation, or corruption detected\")\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tt.Log(\"✓ All Authorization headers passed through ARMOR intact\")\n\tt.Log(\"✓ Header passthrough verified for multiple intact\")\n222\t}\n223\t\n224\t// TestXAmzDateHeaderIntegration tests X-Amz-Date header passthrough\n225\t// through the full request handling pipeline.\n226\tfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderIntegration(t pipeline\")\n290\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Timestamp: %s\", capturedDateHeader)\n291\t\t})\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t// TestXAmzDateHeaderEdgeCases tests edge cases for X-Amz-Date header handling\n295\tfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderEdgeCases(t *testing.T) {\n296\t\ttestCases := []struct {\n297\t\t\tname           string\n298\t\t\txAmzDateHeader string\n299\t\t\tshouldValidate bool\n300\t\t\tdescription    string\n301\t\t}{\n302\t\t\t{\n303\t\t\t\tname:           \"Minimum valid timestamp\",\n304\t\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"19700101T000000Z\",\n305\t\t\t\tshouldValidate: true,\n306\t\t\t\tdescription:    \"Unix epoch timestamp\",\n307\t\t\t},\n308\t\t\t{\n309\t\t\t\tname:           \"Maximum reasonable timestamp\",\n310\t\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"21000101T000000Z\",\n311\t\t\t\tshouldValidate: \"Invalid day 32 (should fail parsing)\",\n337\t\t\t},\n338\t\t\t{\n339\t\t\t\tname:           \"Invalid hour\",\n340\t\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T240000Z\",\n341\t\t\t\tshouldValidate: parsing)\",\n367\t\t\t},\n368\t\t\t{\n369\t\t\t\tname:           \"Lowercase format\",\n370\t\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114t000000z\",\n371\t\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n372\t\t\t\tdescription:    \"Lowercase format (AWS uses uppercase)\",\n373\t\t\t},\n374\t\t}\n375\t\n376\t\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n377\t\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n378\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n379\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Header value: %s\", tc.xAmzDateHeader)\n380\t\n381\t\t\t\t// Attempt to parse the timestamp\n382\t\t\t\t_, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", tc.xAmzDateHeader)\n383\t\n384\t\t\t\tif tc.shouldValidate {\n385\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n386\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected valid timestamp but parsing failed: %v\", err)\n387\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n388\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Valid timestamp format accepted\")\n389\t\t\t\t\t}\n390\t\t\t\t} else {\n391\t\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n392\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected invalid timestamp but parsing succeeded\")\n393\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n394\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Invalid timestamp correctly rejected\")\n395\t\t\t\t\t}\n396\t\t\t\t}\n397\t\t\t})\n398\t\t}\n399\t}\n400\t\n401\t// TestXAmzDateHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing verifies that parsing and\n402\t// rebuilding the X-Amz-Date header produces the same result.\n403\tfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing(t *testing.T) {\n404\t\ttestTimestamps := []string{\n405\t\t\t\"20250114T120000Z\",\n406\t\t\t\"20130524T000000Z\",\n407\t\t\t\"20240229T235959Z\",\n408\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\tfor i, originalTimestamp := that X-Amz-Date headers are passed through\n// to ARMOR intact without modification or corruption.\n//\n// This test ensures that:\n// 1.        \"Timestamp with single-digit hour\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T090000Z\",\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Timestamp with single-digit hour (09:00:00)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: capturedDateHeader)\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date header passed through intact (byte-for-byte match)\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Header length: %d bytes\", headers passed through ARMOR intact\")\n}\n\n// TestXAmzDateHeaderIntegration tests X-Amz-Date header passthrough\n// through the full request handling pipeline.\nfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Create test credentials\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\thandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"X-Amz-Date preserved in full authenticated request\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a properly signed request with X-Amz-Date\n\t\treq := createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n\n\t\t// Capture the X-Amz-Date header before sending\n\t\toriginalDate := req.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Sending request with X-Amz-Date: %s\", originalDate)\n\n\t\t// Create a custom response recorder that captures the request\n\t\tvar capturedDateHeader string\n\n\t\t// Wrap the handler to capture the X-Amz-Date header\n\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\tcapturedDateHeader = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Create response recorder\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t\t// Serve the request through our wrapped handler\n\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify the X-Amz-Date header was preserved\n\t\tif capturedDateHeader != originalDate {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date header modified during request processing\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:  %s\", originalDate)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured: %s\", capturedDateHeader)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify the timestamp format is valid\n\t\t_, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", capturedDateHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Invalid X-Amz-Date timestamp format: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date header preserved through full request pipeline\")\n\t\tt.Logf(\"  Timestamp: %s\", capturedDateHeader)\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestXAmzDateHeaderEdgeCases tests edge cases for X-Amz-Date header handling\nfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderEdgeCases(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\tname           string\n\t\txAmzDateHeader string\n\t\tshouldValidate bool\n\t\tdescription    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Minimum valid timestamp\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"19700101T000000Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: true,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Unix epoch timestamp\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Maximum reasonable timestamp\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"21000101T000000Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: true,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Year 2100 (far future)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Leap second at end of June\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20150630T235960Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Leap second (June 30, 2015) - valid in AWS but Go's time.Parse doesn't support it\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Leap second at end of December\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20161231T235960Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Leap second (December 31, 2016) - valid in AWS but Go's time.Parse doesn't support it\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Invalid month\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20251301T000000Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Invalid month 13 (should fail parsing)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Invalid day\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250132T000000Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Invalid day 32 (should fail parsing)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Invalid hour\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T240000Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Invalid hour 24 (should fail parsing)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Invalid minute\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T006000Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Invalid minute 60 (should fail parsing)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Invalid second (non-leap)\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T000061Z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Invalid second 61 (should fail parsing)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Missing timezone indicator\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T000000\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Missing 'Z' timezone indicator (should fail parsing)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Wrong timezone indicator\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T000000+00:00\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Non-standard timezone format (should fail parsing)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Lowercase format\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114t000000z\",\n\t\t\tshouldValidate: false,\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Lowercase format (AWS uses uppercase)\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Header value: %s\", tc.xAmzDateHeader)\n\n\t\t\t// Attempt to parse the timestamp\n\t\t\t_, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", tc.xAmzDateHeader)\n\n\t\t\tif tc.shouldValidate {\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected valid timestamp but parsing failed: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Valid timestamp format accepted\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected invalid timestamp but parsing succeeded\")\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Invalid timestamp correctly rejected\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestXAmzDateHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing verifies that parsing and\n// rebuilding the X-Amz-Date header produces the same result.\nfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestTimestamps := []string{\n\t\t\"20250114T120000Z\",\n\t\t\"20130524T000000Z\",\n\t\t\"20240229T235959Z\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, originalTimestamp := range testTimestamps {\n\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"Round-trip test %d\", i+1), func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Parse the timestamp\n\t\t\tparsedTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", originalTimestamp)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse timestamp: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Reconstruct the timestamp from the parsed time\n\t\t\treconstructed := parsedTime.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\")\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the reconstructed timestamp matches the original\n\t\t\tif reconstructed != originalTimestamp {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Timestamp changed during round-trip\")\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:     %s\", originalTimestamp)\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Reconstructed: %s\", reconstructed)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Timestamp unchanged through parse/format cycle\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Value: %s\", reconstructed)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestXAmzDateHeaderFormatPreservation verifies that the X-Amz-Date header\n// format is preserved exactly as specified by AWS.\nfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderFormatPreservation(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\tname           string\n\t\txAmzDateHeader string\n\t\texpectedFormat string\n\t\tdescription    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Standard format with T separator\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"20250114T120000Z\",\n\t\t\texpectedFormat: \"YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ\",\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Standard AWS format with 'T' separator\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"All zeros timestamp\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"00000101T000000Z\",\n\t\t\texpectedFormat: \"YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ\",\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Minimum timestamp value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"Maximum values\",\n\t\t\txAmzDateHeader: \"99991231T235959Z\",\n\t\t\texpectedFormat: \"YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ\",\n\t\t\tdescription:    \"Maximum reasonable timestamp\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Header: %s\", tc.xAmzDateHeader)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the header length is exactly 16 characters\n\t\t\tif len(tc.xAmzDateHeader) != 16 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Invalid X-Amz-Date length: expected 16, got %d\", len(tc.xAmzDateHeader))\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the format structure: YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ\n\t\t\tif tc.xAmzDateHeader[8] != 'T' {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Missing 'T' separator at position 8\")\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif tc.xAmzDateHeader[15] != 'Z' {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Missing 'Z' timezone indicator at position 15\")\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify all other characters are digits\n\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < 16; i++ {\n\t\t\t\tif i == 8 || i == 15 {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip 'T' and 'Z'\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif tc.xAmzDateHeader[i] < '0' || tc.xAmzDateHeader[i] > '9' {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Non-digit character at position %d: %c\", i, tc.xAmzDateHeader[i])\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date format preserved: %s\", tc.expectedFormat)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Structure: YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Length: 16 characters\")\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestXAmzDateHeaderTimeZones verifies timezone handling in X-Amz-Date headers.\n// AWS requires all timestamps to be in UTC (indicated by 'Z').\nfunc TestXAmzDateHeaderTimeZones(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\tname           string\n\t\txAmzDateHeader string\n\t\tshouldParse    bool\n\t\tdescription tc.description)\n242\t\n243\t\t\t\t// Create request with the S3 headers\n244\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n245\t\n246\t\t\t\t// Set all the test headers\n247\t\t\t\tfor headerName, headerValue := range tc.headers {\n248\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(headerName, headerValue)\n249\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Setting %s: %s\", headerName, Create a custom response recorder that captures the request\n261\t\t\t\tcapturedHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n262\t\t\t\tvar headersCaptured bool\n263\t\n264\t\t\t\t// Wrap the handler to capture the S3 headers as ARMOR receives them\n265\t\t\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n266\t\t\t\t\t// Capture all the S3 headers as ARMOR receives them\n267\t\t\t\t\tfor _, headerName := range tc.validateHeaders {\n268\t\t\t\t\t\tif value := r.Header.Get(headerName); value != \"\" {\n269\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeaders[headerName] = value\n270\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n271\t\t\t\t\t}\n272\t\t\t\t\theadersCaptured = true\n273\t\n274\t\t\t\t\t// Call the original handler\n275\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n276\t\t\t\t})\n277\t\n278\t\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n279\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n280\t\n281\t\t\t\t// Serve the request through our wrapped handler\n282\t\t\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n283\t\n284\t\t\t\t// Verify we captured the S3 headers\n285\t\t\t\tif !headersCaptured {\n286\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture S3 headers from request\")\n287\t\t\t\t\treturn\n288\t\t\t\t}\n289\t\n290\t\t\t\t// Verify each captured header matches the original exactly (byte-for-byte)\n291\t\t\t\tallMatched := true\n292\t\t\t\tfor _, headerName := range tc.validateHeaders {\n293\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue, originalExists := originalHeaders[headerName]\n294\t\t\t\t\tcapturedValue, capturedExists := capturedHeaders[headerName]\n295\t\n296\t\t\t\t\tif !originalExists {\n297\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Original header %s was not set\", headerName)\n298\t\t\t\t\t\tallMatched = false\n299\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n300\t\t\t\t\t}\n301\t\n302\t\t\t\t\tif !capturedExists {\n303\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Captured header %s is missing\", headerName)\n304\t\t\t\t\t\tallMatched = false\n305\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n306\t\t\t\t\t}\n307\t\n308\t\t\t\t\tif capturedValue != originalValue {\n309\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s header was modified during passthrough!\", headerName)\n310\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original length: %d\", len(originalValue))\n311\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured length: %d\", len(capturedValue))\n312\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:  %q\", originalValue)\n313\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured: %q\", capturedValue)\n314\t\n315\t\t\t\t\t\t// Find the first difference\n316\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen := len(originalValue)\n317\t\t\t\t\t\tif len(capturedValue) < minLen {\n318\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(capturedValue)\n319\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n320\t\t\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n321\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif originalValue[i] != capturedValue[i] {\n322\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"First difference at byte %d: original[%d]=%c (0x%02x), captured[%d]=%c (0x%02x)\",\n323\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\ti, i, originalValue[i], originalValue[i],\n324\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\ti, capturedValue[i], capturedValue[i])\n325\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n326\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n327\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n328\t\t\t\t\t\tallMatched = false\n329\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n330\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ %s preserved exactly (length: %d bytes)\", headerName, len(capturedValue))\n331\t\t\t\t\t}\n332\t\t\t\t}\n333\t\n334\t\t\t\tif allMatched {\n335\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ All S3 headers passed through intact (byte-for-byte match)\")\n336\t\t\t\t} else {\n337\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Some S3 headers were modified during passthrough\")\n338\t\t\t\t}\n339\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n340\t\t\t})\n341\t\t}\n342\t\n343\t\tt.Log(\"✓ S3-specific headers preservation test completed\")\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t//   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\thandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"Complete SigV4 request with all S3 header\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20260715/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, \"TESTACCESSKEY/20260715/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\":   \"host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256\",\n\t\t\t\"Host\":                  \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\",\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor headerName, headerValue := range s3Headers {\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(headerName, headerValue)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing complete SigV4 request with all S3 headers\")\n\n\t\t// Store the original headers\n\t\toriginalHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n\t\tfor headerName := range s3Headers {\n\t\t\tif value := req.Header.Get(headerName); value != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders[headerName] = value\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\toriginalAuth := req.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\n\t\t// Capture the headers as ARMOR receives them\n\t\tcapturedHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n\t\tvar capturedAuth string\n\n\t\t// Wrap the handler to capture headers\n\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t// Capture Authorization header\n\t\t\tcapturedAuth = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\n\t\t\t// Capture all S3 headers\n\t\t\tfor headerName := range s3Headers {\n\t\t\t\tif value := r.Header.Get(headerName); value != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeaders[headerName] = value\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Create response recorder and serve request\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify Authorization header was preserved\n\t\tif capturedAuth != originalAuth {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization header was modified during passthrough\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:  %q\", truncateForLog(originalAuth))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured: %q\", truncateForLog(capturedAuth))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify all S3 headers were preserved\n\t\tallS3HeadersPreserved := true\n\t\tfor headerName, originalValue := range originalHeaders {\n\t\t\tcapturedValue := capturedHeaders[headerName]\n\t\t\tif capturedValue != originalValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s header was modified\", headerName)\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:  %q\", originalValue)\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured: %q\", capturedValue)\n\t\t\t\tallS3HeadersPreserved = false\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ %s preserved\", headerName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif allS3HeadersPreserved {\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Complete SigV4 request passed through intact\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"SigV4 request with session token (X-Amz-Security-Token)\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a SigV4 request with temporary credentials (session token)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\n\t\t// Set Authorization header for session credentials\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20260715/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, \"TESTACCESSKEY/20260715/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\":   \"host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256;x-amz-security-token\",\n\t\t\t\"Host\":                  \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\",\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor headerName, headerValue := range s3Headers {\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(headerName, headerValue)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing SigV4 request with session token\")\n\n\t\t// Store the original headers\n\t\toriginalHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n\t\tfor headerName := range s3Headers {\n\t\t\tif value := req.Header.Get(headerName); value != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders[headerName] = value\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Capture the headers as ARMOR receives them\n\t\tcapturedHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n\n\t\t// Wrap the handler to r.Header.Get(headerName); value != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeaders[headerName] = value\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Create response recorder and serve request\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify all S3 headers including security token were preserved\n\t\tallHeadersPreserved := true\n\t\tfor headerName, originalValue := range originalHeaders {\n\t\t\tcapturedValue := capturedHeaders[headerName]\n\t\t\tif capturedValue != originalValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s header was modified\", headerName)\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:  %q\", truncateForLog(originalValue))\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured: %q\", truncateForLog(capturedValue))\n\t\t\t\tallHeadersPreserved = false\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ %s preserved\", headerName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif allHeadersPreserved {\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Session token request passed through intact\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// occurs\",\n142\t\t\t\tvalidateHeaders: []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\"},\n143\t\t\t},\n144\t\t}\n145\t\n146\t\tt.Log(\"Testing Multi-Hop Header Preservation\")\n147\t\tt.Log(\"======================================\")\n148\t\tt.Log(\"This test simulates requests passing through multiple intermediate\")\n149\t\tt.Log(\"proxies (Cloudflare, load balancers, etc.) before reaching ARMOR.\")\n150\t\tt.Log(\"It verifies that authentication headers survive intact through all hops.\")\n151\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n152\t\n153\t\tfor _, scenario := range testScenarios {\n154\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n155\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Scenario: %s\", scenario.description)\n156\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Hops: %d\", len(scenario.hops))\n157\t\n158\t\t\t\t// Build the handler chain: hops → ARMOR\n159\t\t\t\thandler := armorHandler\n160\t\t\t\tfor i := len(scenario.hops) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n161\t\t\t\t\thandler = scenario.hops[i](handler)\n162\t\t\t\t}\n163\t\n164\t\t\t\tfor _, tc := range headerTestCases {\n165\t\t\t\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n166\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n167\t\n168\t\t\t\t\t\t// Create request with authentication headers\n169\t\t\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n170\t\t\t\t\t\tfor key, value := range tc.headers {\n171\t\t\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n172\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n173\t\n174\t\t\t\t\t\t// Store original headers for comparison\n175\t\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n176\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n177\t\t\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders[key] = req.Header.Get(key)\n178\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n179\t\n180\t\t\t\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n181\t\t\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n182\t\n183\t\t\t\t\t\t// Create a custom wrapper to capture headers at ARMOR's boundary\n184\t\t\t\t\t\tvar capturedHeadersAtArmor map[string]string\n185\t\t\t\t\t\tvar authCaptured bool\n186\t\n187\t\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n188\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Capture headers as they arrive at ARMOR (after all hops)\n189\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor = make(map[string]string)\n190\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n191\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor[key] = r.Header.Get(key)\n192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n193\t\t\t\t\t\t\tauthCaptured = true\n194\t\n195\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Call the actual handler\n196\t\t\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n197\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n198\t\n199\t\t\t\t\t\t// Serve the request through the multi-hop chain\n200\t\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n201\t\n202\t\t\t\t\t\t// Verify we captured headers at ARMOR boundary\n203\t\t\t\t\t\tif !authCaptured {\n204\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture headers at ARMOR boundary\")\n205\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n206\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n207\t\n208\t\t\t\t\t\t// Validate each critical header\n209\t\t\t\t\t\tallPassed := true\n210\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n211\t\t\t\t\t\t\toriginal := originalHeaders[key]\n212\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcaptured := capturedHeadersAtArmor[key]\n213\t\n214\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original == \"\" && captured == \"\" {\n215\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Header was not sent, skip validation\n216\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n217\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original != captured {\n220\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Header %q was corrupted during multi-hop passthrough!\", key)\n221\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original length:  %d bytes\", len(original))\n222\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured length:  %d bytes\", len(captured))\n223\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original value:   %q\", truncateForLog(original))\n224\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured value:   %q\", truncateForLog(captured))\n225\t\n226\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Find first difference\n227\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen := len(original)\n228\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif len(captured) < minLen {\n229\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(captured)\n230\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n231\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n232\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original[i] != captured[i] {\n233\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  First difference at byte %d:\", i)\n234\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    original[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, original[i], original[i])\n235\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    captured[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, captured[i], captured[i])\n236\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n237\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n238\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n239\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tallPassed = false\n240\t\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n241\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Header %q preserved intact (%d bytes)\", key, len(captured))\n242\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n244\t\n245\t\t\t\t\t\tif allPassed {\n246\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ All headers survived %d-hop journey intact\", len(scenario.hops))\n247\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n248\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n249\t\t\t\t\t})\n250\t\t\t\t}\n251\t\t\t})\n252\t\t}\n253\t\n254\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Multi-hop header preservation verified\")\n255\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Authorization and X-Amz-* headers survive through intermediate proxies\")\n256\t}\n257\t\n258\t// TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior tests multi-hop scenarios with more\n259\t// realistic proxy behavior that might strip or modify headers.\n260\tfunc TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior(t *testing.T) {\n261\t\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n262\t\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n263\t\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n264\t\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n265\t\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n266\t\t\t},\n267\t\t}\n268\t\n269\t\tcfg := &config.Config{\n270\t\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n271\t\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n272\t\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n273\t\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n274\t\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n275\t\t}\n276\t\n277\t\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n278\t\tif err != nil {\n279\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n293\t\t\t\t\t// Simulate Cloudflare adding its own headers\n294\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-RAY\", \"1234567890abcdef\")\n295\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-IPCountry\", \"US\")\n296\t\n297\t\t\t\t\t// Cloudflare preserves auth headers (this is the critical behavior)\n298\t\t\t\t\t// In reality, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n310\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n311\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n314\t\n315\t\t\t// Capture headers at ARMOR boundary\n316\t\t\tvar capturedAuth, capturedAmzDate, capturedAmzSha string\n317\t\t\tcapturingHandler := httptest.NewRecorder()\n325\t\t\tcapturingHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n326\t\n327\t\t\t// Verify headers are preserved\n328\t\t\tif capturedAuth != authHeader {\n329\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization {\n333\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n334\t\t\t}\n335\t\n336\t\t\tif capturedAmzDate != \"20130524T000000Z\" {\n337\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date header X-Amz-Content-Sha256 header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n346\t\t\t}\n347\t\n348\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Realistic Cloudflare proxy preserves auth headers correctly\")\n349\t\t})\n350\t\n351\t\tt.Run(\"Load balancer that preserves authentication headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n352\t\t\tt.Log(\"Testing load balancer behavior:\")\n353\t\t\tt.Log(\"- Load balancer adds X-Forwarded-* load balancer layer\n358\t\t\tloadBalancer := func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n359\t\t\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n360\t\t\t\t\t// Simulate load balancer adding forwarding headers\n361\t\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-For\", \"203.0.113.1\")\n362\t\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Proto\", compatibility\n367\t\n368\t\t\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n369\t\t\t\t})\n370\t\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\t\thandler := loadBalancer(armorHandler)\n373\t\n374\t\t\t// Create request with auth SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n377\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n378\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n379\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n380\t\n381\t\t\t// Capture headers at ARMOR boundary\n382\t\t\tvar capturedAuth, capturedAmzDate string\n383\t\t\tcapturingHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n384\t\t\t\tcapturedAuth = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n385\t\t\t\tcapturedAmzDate = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n386\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n387\t\t\t})\n388\t\n389\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n390\t\t\tcapturingHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n391\t\n392\t\t\t// Verify headers are preserved\n393\t\t\tif capturedAuth != authHeader {\n394\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization header corrupted by load balancer\")\n395\t\t\t} else {\n396\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved through load balancer\")\n397\t\t\t}\n398\t\n399\t\t\tif capturedAmzDate != \"20130524T000000Z\" {\n400\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date header corrupted by load balancer\")\n401\t\t\t} else {\n402\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date header preserved through load balancer\n472\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n473\t\t\t})\n474\t\n475\t\t\t// Wrap to capture after Load Balancer\n476\t\t\tcaptureAfterLB := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n477\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB = make(map[string]string)\n478\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n479\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n480\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n481\t\t\t\tcapturedLB = true\n482\t\n483\t\t\t\t// Continue to ARMOR\n484\t\t\t\tcaptureAfterCF.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n485\t\t\t})\n486\t\n487\t\t\t// Wrap to capture at ARMOR boundary\n488\t\t\tcaptureAtArmor := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\", \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"}\n525\t\t\tallPreserved := true\n526\t\n527\t\t\tfor _, headerName := range allHeaders {\n528\t\t\t\tvar originalValue string\n529\t\t\t\tswitch headerName {\n530\t\t\t\tcase \"Authorization\":\n531\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAuth\n532\t\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Date\":\n533\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzDate\n534\t\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\":\n535\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzSha\n536\t\t\t\t}\n537\t\n538\t\t\t\tcfValue := headersAfterCF[headerName]\n539\t\t\t\tlbValue := headersAfterLB[headerName]\n540\t\t\t\tarmorValue := headersAtArmor[headerName]\n541\t\n542\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  %s:\", headerName)\n543\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    Original: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(originalValue), len(originalValue))\n544\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After CF: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(cfValue), len(cfValue))\n545\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After LB: %q (len=%d)\", lbValue && lbValue == armorValue {\n549\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✓ Preserved through all hops\")\n550\t\t\t\t} else {\n551\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✗ CORRUPTED during path\")\n561\t\t\t}\n562\t\t})\n563\t}\n564\t\n565\t// Helper types and functions for multi-hop testing\n566\t\n567\t// hopLayer represents a middleware layer in the request path\n568\ttype hopLayer func(next http.Handler) http.Handler\n569\t\n570\t// cloudflareProxyLayer simulates Cloudflare as a reverse proxy\n571\t// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers but adds its own metadata headers\n572\tfunc cloudflareProxyLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n573\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n574\t\t\t// Cloudflare adds response headers\n575\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-RAY\", \"1234567890abcdef\")\n576\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-Cache-Status\", \"MISS\")\n577\t\n578\t\t\t// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers (critical behavior)\n579\t\t\t// Cloudflare does NOT strip Authorization, X-Amz-* headers\n580\t\n581\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n582\t\t})\n583\t}\n584\t\n585\t// loadBalancerLayer simulates a layer 7 load balancer\n586\t// Load balancers typically preserve authentication headers for S3 API compatibility\n587\tfunc loadBalancerLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n588\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n589\t\t\t// Load balancers add forwarding headers\n590\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-For\", \"203.0.113.1\")\n591\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Proto\", \"https\")\n592\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Host\", \"armor.example.com\")\n593\t\n594\t\t\t// Load balancers preserve authentication headers (critical for S3 APIs)\n595\t\n596\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n597\t\t})\n598\t}\n599\t\n600\t// reverseProxyLayer simulates a generic reverse proxy (nginx, envoy, etc.)\n601\tfunc reverseProxyLayer(name string) hopLayer {\n602\t\treturn func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n603\t\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n604\t\t\t\t// Generic reverse proxy behavior\n605\t\t\t\t// Most reverse proxies preserve authentication headers by default\n606\t\t\t\t// unless explicitly configured to strip them\n607\t\n608\t\t\t\t// Add proxy identification header\n609\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-By\", name)\n610\t\n611\t\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n612\t\t\t})\n613\t\t}\n614\t}\n615\t\n616\t// TestMultiHopHeaderIntegrityUnderLoad {\n698\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(err)\n699\t\t\t\t\terrorCount++\n700\t\t\t\t}\n701\t\t\t}\n702\t\n703\t\t\tif errorCount == 0 {\n704\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ All %d concurrent requests preserved headers correctly\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Credential\":        \"TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\":     \"host;x-amz-content-sha256;x-amz-date\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Host\":                    \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tdescription:     \"Full SigV4 header suite with multiple X-Amz-* headers\",\n\t\t\tvalidateHeaders: []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\", \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\", \"X-Amz-Algorithm\", \"X-Amz-Credential\", \"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"Long Authorization header\",\n\t\t\theaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\"Authorization\": \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=content-type;host;x-amz-content-sha256;x-amz-date;x-amz-security-token;x-amz-user-agent, Signature=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Date\":    \"20130524T000000Z\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Host\":          \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tdescription:     \"Long signature to test no truncation occurs\",\n\t\t\tvalidateHeaders: []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\"},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tt.Log(\"Testing Multi-Hop Header Preservation\")\n\tt.Log(\"======================================\")\n\tt.Log(\"This test simulates requests passing through multiple intermediate\")\n\tt.Log(\"proxies (Cloudflare, load balancers, etc.) before reaching ARMOR.\")\n\tt.Log(\"It verifies that authentication headers survive intact through all hops.\")\n\tt.Log(\"\")\n\n\tfor _, scenario := range testScenarios {\n\t\tt.Run(scenario.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Scenario: %s\", scenario.description)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Hops: %d\", len(scenario.hops))\n\n\t\t\t// Build the handler chain: hops → ARMOR\n\t\t\thandler := armorHandler\n\t\t\tfor i := len(scenario.hops) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n\t\t\t\thandler = scenario.hops[i](handler)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tfor _, tc := range headerTestCases {\n\t\t\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Create request with authentication headers\n\t\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\t\t\t\tfor key, value := range tc.headers {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Store original headers for comparison\n\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n\t\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders[key] = req.Header.Get(key)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n\t\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Create a custom wrapper to capture headers at ARMOR's boundary\n\t\t\t\t\tvar capturedHeadersAtArmor map[string]string\n\t\t\t\t\tvar authCaptured bool\n\n\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Capture headers as they arrive at ARMOR (after all hops)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor[key] = r.Header.Get(key)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tauthCaptured = true\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Call the actual handler\n\t\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Serve the request through the multi-hop chain\n\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Verify we captured headers at ARMOR boundary\n\t\t\t\t\tif !authCaptured {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture headers at ARMOR boundary\")\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate each critical header\n\t\t\t\t\tallPassed := true\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n\t\t\t\t\t\toriginal := originalHeaders[key]\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcaptured := capturedHeadersAtArmor[key]\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif original == \"\" && captured == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Header was not sent, skip validation\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif original != captured {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Header %q was corrupted during multi-hop passthrough!\", key)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original length:  %d bytes\", len(original))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured length:  %d bytes\", len(captured))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original value:   %q\", truncateForLog(original))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured value:   %q\", truncateForLog(captured))\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Find first difference\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen := len(original)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif len(captured) < minLen {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(captured)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original[i] != captured[i] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  First difference at byte %d:\", i)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    original[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, original[i], original[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    captured[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, captured[i], captured[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tallPassed = false\n\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Header %q preserved intact (%d bytes)\", key, len(captured))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif allPassed {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ All headers survived %d-hop journey intact\", len(scenario.hops))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tt.Log(\"✓ Multi-hop header preservation verified\")\n\tt.Log(\"✓ Authorization and X-Amz-* headers survive through intermediate proxies\")\n}\n\n// TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior tests multi-hop scenarios with more\n// realistic proxy behavior that might strip or modify headers.\nfunc TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior(t *testing.T) {\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tarmorHandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"Realistic Cloudflare proxy with header passthrough\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tt.Log(\"Testing realistic Cloudflare proxy behavior:\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Cloudflare adds CF-RAY, CF-IPCountry headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Cloudflare preserves Authorization and X-Amz-* headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Request path: Client → Cloudflare → ARMOR\")\n\n\t\t// Create realistic Cloudflare proxy layer\n\t\trealisticCloudflare := func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\t\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t// Simulate Cloudflare adding its own headers\n\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-RAY\", \"1234567890abcdef\")\n\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-IPCountry\", \"US\")\n\n\t\t\t\t// Cloudflare preserves auth headers (this is the critical behavior)\n\t\t\t\t// In reality, Cloudflare does NOT strip Authorization or X-Amz-* headers\n\n\t\t\t\t// Call next handler\n\t\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\thandler := realisticCloudflare(armorHandler)\n\n\t\t// Create request with full S3 auth headers\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\", \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t// Capture headers at ARMOR boundary\n\t\tvar capturedAuth, capturedAmzDate, capturedAmzSha string\n\t\tcapturingHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\tcapturedAuth = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\tcapturedAmzDate = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\tcapturedAmzSha = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tcapturingHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify headers are preserved\n\t\tif capturedAuth != authHeader {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization header corrupted by Cloudflare proxy\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Expected: %q\", truncateForLog(authHeader))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Got:      %q\", truncateForLog(capturedAuth))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif capturedAmzDate != \"20130524T000000Z\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date header corrupted by Cloudflare proxy\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif capturedAmzSha != \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256 header corrupted by Cloudflare proxy\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Content-Sha256 header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Realistic Cloudflare proxy preserves auth headers correctly\")\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Load balancer that preserves authentication headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tt.Log(\"Testing load balancer behavior:\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Load balancer adds X-Forwarded-* headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Load balancer preserves Authorization and X-Amz-* headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Request path: Client → Load Balancer → ARMOR\")\n\n\t\t// Create realistic load balancer layer\n\t\tloadBalancer := func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\t\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t// Simulate load balancer adding forwarding headers\n\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-For\", \"203.0.113.1\")\n\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Proto\", \"https\")\n\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Host\", \"armor.example.com\")\n\n\t\t\t\t// Load balancers typically preserve auth headers\n\t\t\t\t// This is critical for S3 API compatibility\n\n\t\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\thandler := loadBalancer(armorHandler)\n\n\t\t// Create request with auth headers\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t// Capture headers at ARMOR boundary\n\t\tvar capturedAuth, capturedAmzDate string\n\t\tcapturingHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\tcapturedAuth = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\tcapturedAmzDate = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tcapturingHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify headers are preserved\n\t\tif capturedAuth != authHeader {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization header corrupted by load balancer\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved through load balancer\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif capturedAmzDate != \"20130524T000000Z\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date header corrupted by load balancer\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date header preserved through load balancer\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify X-Forwarded headers were added\n\t\tif req.Header.Get(\"X-Forwarded-For\") != \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Load balancer added X-Forwarded headers correctly\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Load balancer preserves auth headers correctly\")\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestMultiHopEndToEndHeaderFidelity tests complete end-to-end header fidelity\n// through the entire request path, simulating a real production environment.\nfunc TestMultiHopEndToEndHeaderFidelity(t *testing.T) {\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tarmorHandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"Complete production path: Client → Cloudflare → Load Balancer → ARMOR\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tt.Log(\"Testing complete production request path:\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"1. Client creates S3 request with Authorization header\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"2. Request passes through Cloudflare (CDN/reverse proxy)\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"3. Request passes through load balancer (L7 LB)\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"4. Request reaches ARMOR S3 API server\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"5. Verify headers are byte-for-byte identical at each step\")\n\n\t\t// Build the complete proxy chain\n\t\thandler := armorHandler\n\n\t\t// Layer 3: Load Balancer (closest to ARMOR)\n\t\thandler = loadBalancerLayer(handler)\n\n\t\t// Layer 2: Cloudflare (in front of load balancer)\n\t\thandler = cloudflareProxyLayer(handler)\n\n\t\t// Capture headers at each hop\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF, headersAfterLB, headersAtArmor map[string]string\n\t\t\tcapturedCF, capturedLB, capturedArmor bool\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// Wrap to capture after Cloudflare\n\t\tcaptureAfterCF := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\tcapturedCF = true\n\n\t\t\t// Continue to load balancer\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Wrap to capture after Load Balancer\n\t\tcaptureAfterLB := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\tcapturedLB = true\n\n\t\t\t// Continue to ARMOR\n\t\t\tcaptureAfterCF.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Wrap to capture at ARMOR boundary\n\t\tcaptureAtArmor := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\tcapturedArmor = true\n\n\t\t\t// Call ARMOR\n\t\t\tcaptureAfterLB.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Create client request with full S3 auth\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\toriginalAuth := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n\t\toriginalAmzDate := \"20130524T000000Z\"\n\t\toriginalAmzSha := \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\"\n\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", originalAuth)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", originalAmzDate)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\", originalAmzSha)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t// Serve the complete request chain\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tcaptureAtArmor.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify we captured at all points\n\t\tif !capturedCF || !capturedLB || !capturedArmor {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture headers at all hop points\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"capturedCF: %v, capturedLB: %v, capturedArmor: %v\", capturedCF, capturedLB, capturedArmor)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify end-to-end fidelity\n\t\tt.Log(\"Verifying end-to-end header fidelity:\")\n\n\t\tallHeaders := []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\", \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"}\n\t\tallPreserved := true\n\n\t\tfor _, headerName := range allHeaders {\n\t\t\tvar originalValue string\n\t\t\tswitch headerName {\n\t\t\tcase \"Authorization\":\n\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAuth\n\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Date\":\n\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzDate\n\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\":\n\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzSha\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tcfValue := headersAfterCF[headerName]\n\t\t\tlbValue := headersAfterLB[headerName]\n\t\t\tarmorValue := headersAtArmor[headerName]\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  %s:\", headerName)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    Original: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(originalValue), len(originalValue))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After CF: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(cfValue), len(cfValue))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After LB: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(lbValue), len(lbValue))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    At ARMOR: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(armorValue), len(armorValue))\n\n\t\t\tif originalValue == cfValue && cfValue == lbValue && lbValue == armorValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✓ Preserved through all hops\")\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✗ CORRUPTED during passthrough!\")\n\t\t\t\tallPreserved = false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif allPreserved {\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ End-to-end header fidelity verified\")\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ All headers preserved byte-for-byte through complete production path\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"✗ Header corruption detected in multi-hop path\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// Helper types and functions for multi-hop testing\n\n// hopLayer represents a middleware layer in the request path\ntype hopLayer func(next http.Handler) http.Handler\n\n// cloudflareProxyLayer simulates Cloudflare as a reverse proxy\n// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers but adds its own metadata headers\nfunc cloudflareProxyLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t// Cloudflare adds response headers\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-RAY\", \"1234567890abcdef\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-Cache-Status\", \"MISS\")\n\n\t\t// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers (critical behavior)\n\t\t// Cloudflare does NOT strip Authorization, X-Amz-* headers\n\n\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t})\n}\n\n// loadBalancerLayer simulates a layer 7 load balancer\n// Load balancers typically preserve authentication headers for S3 API compatibility\nfunc loadBalancerLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t// Load balancers add forwarding headers\n\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-For\", \"203.0.113.1\")\n\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Proto\", \"https\")\n\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Host\", \"armor.example.com\")\n\n\t\t// Load balancers preserve authentication headers (critical for S3 APIs)\n\n\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t})\n}\n\n// reverseProxyLayer simulates a generic reverse proxy (nginx, envoy, etc.)\nfunc reverseProxyLayer(name string) hopLayer {\n\treturn func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) identification header\n\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-By\", name)\n\n\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMultiHopHeaderIntegrityUnderLoad tests header preservation when\n// multiple
